canonical.title: Reframing the AI & Security talk for a non-technical audience

siblings.title: Title candidates
siblings.summary: Eight title candidates for the laymen reframing of the AI-Security-Talk, with a recommended pick and the patterns behind each.

siblings.title: Existing deck triage
siblings.summary: Slide-by-slide keep/trim/simplify/cut verdict on the AI-Security-Talk deck for a non-technical audience, plus the four laymen-relevant topics to graft in.

siblings.title: AI vs LLM and other foundational concepts
siblings.summary: A non-technical primer on the AI/ML/deep-learning/LLM stack and the surrounding jargon.

siblings.title: Scams, deepfakes, and social-engineering in 2026
siblings.summary: Field guide to the 2026 AI scam landscape — named cases, dollar losses, attacker tooling, and the four defense layers.

siblings.title: Data harvesting and privacy
siblings.summary: AI runs on harvested data. The legal wins are mostly piracy cases, not privacy ones.

siblings.title: Ads, manipulation, and trust erosion
siblings.summary: The ad-funded internet was already a manipulation engine; AI cheapens every step.

siblings.title: Misinformation, hallucination harms, and election/medical/legal risks
siblings.summary: Where LLM hallucinations have caused measurable harm — courts, hospitals, elections.

siblings.title: Presentation craft for non-technical audiences
siblings.summary: A CEO-depth playbook for explaining technical work to non-technical audiences.
